what_it_does: A Workshop multi-step flow builder. Defines wizard kinds (property/onboarding/generic) each with a canonical shape and seeded steps, step kinds (capture/form/choice/review/celebrate), and layouts (sidebar/progress/cards). Provides a Vi drafter (viDraftWizard) that turns a brief into wizard JSON, the WizardBuilder editor (step strip with reorder/add/remove, layout picker, per-step editor with branching rules, candidate gallery), a WizardRender that draws the flow in any of the three layouts, an interactive prototype mode with persisted per-step state and choice-driven branching, a per-step Vi refine, shape glyphs, and JSX/PDF export.
observations: Each wizard kind carries a canonical shape (property→hexagon, onboarding→circle, generic→diamond); the Vi prompt also states the broader shape vocabulary (Virtual Hub→octagon, Vi→diamond, User Portal→circle)
viDraftWizard and ViStepRefine both inject voice.conceptv (or restate the voice rules inline in ViStepRefine) and use CV_AI.complete with a brace-regex JSON fallback, throwing if unparsable
Prototype mode preserves field state across steps (protoState keyed by step id) and supports choice branching via step.gotoOnChoice ({option→ 'next'|'end'|index})
WizardRender renders three distinct layout shells (sidebar rail, top progress bar, card stack) sharing one StepBody
ShapeGlyph draws hexagon/diamond/circle inline SVG using token-var stroke/fill
Branching dropdown reads other steps via window.WS_AI.bridge.getActive().steps
Export builds a standalone self-contained JSX wizard (own useState navigation) via wizardToJSX, plus copy/print
standouts: Choice-step branching (gotoOnChoice) makes the prototype a real navigable flow, not just linear next/back
Canonical shape-per-kind vocabulary tying flows to a consistent geometric identity
The exported standalone JSX is a working interactive wizard, not just a static snapshot
conventions: Follows the window-global registry/export pattern and sources the voice from CV_AI rather than inlining — except ViStepRefine which restates the voice rules inline in its prompt
Uses CSS var() tokens for the in-app rendering
Breaks the no-raw-px rule heavily in the exported JSX template (many hardcoded px) and in some inline styles
concerns: ViStepRefine inlines the brand-voice rules ('sentence case, second person, no exclamation marks, no emoji') instead of pulling voice.conceptv — a second home for the voice, the exact drift the project forbids
Dead/no-op disabled logic on the Continue button: `disabled={!proto && idx < total - 1 ? false : false}` always evaluates to false
The exported wizard JSX reimplements the layout with hardcoded px literals — a parallel rendering path divergent from the in-app renderer
Math.random()-based step ids could collide in rare cases (short 5-char suffix)
notes: The Workshop wizard editor — three flow archetypes with canonical shapes, three layouts, an interactive prototype with branching, and a standalone-JSX export; note one helper (ViStepRefine) inlines the voice rules instead of sourcing them.
questions: Why does ViStepRefine restate voice rules inline when viDraftWizard sources voice.conceptv?
Is the gotoOnChoice keyed by option label or option index — the editor writes by label but onAdvance reads protoState._pick which holds the label?
What does WS_AI.bridge.getActive() return and how does it relate to the current doc?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches — header says a multi-step flow builder (property/onboarding/generic) with visual-mock and functional-prototype modes, and the code delivers all of that plus branching and export.
apparent_themes: Archetype-driven generation (kinds seed canonical steps + shape)
AI-as-collaborator (Vi drafts whole flows, refines steps, proposes alternatives)
Mock vs functional-prototype duality with real state
Single-source design via tokens and shared shape vocabulary
intention_signals: Aims to let a designer assemble or have Vi draft a multi-step flow from named archetypes, preview it in multiple layouts, exercise it as a real branching prototype with persisted state, and export it as a working standalone component — keeping shape identity and brand voice consistent.
novelty: Choice-driven branching in a mock/prototype flow builder plus the canonical shape-per-wizard-kind identity, and an export that emits a fully interactive (not static) wizard component.
contribution: Contributes a flow/wizard authoring canvas: a wizard-kind+step-kind+layout taxonomy with seeded archetypes and canonical shapes, a Vi flow drafter and per-step refiner, a three-layout renderer with an interactive branching prototype and persisted state, and a standalone interactive-JSX export.
summary_for_embedding: A ConceptV Workshop wizard builder with property/onboarding/generic archetypes, capture/form/choice/review/celebrate step kinds, three layouts, choice branching, an interactive prototype with persisted state, Vi drafting/refinement, and standalone-JSX export.
intention_for_embedding: To author or have Vi draft multi-step flows from canonical archetypes, preview them across layouts, run them as real branching prototypes, and export a working interactive component, keeping shape and voice consistent.
